mutants.description: Remove the positive public coverage contribution.

sample_disagreements.label_note: Recover is a no go here, you will be outdamaged by super effective moves. Go ahead and get chip on it so Lapras can come in and kill with an ice move after a free switch in from Starmie fainting.

mutants.description: Remove the public Psychic-into-Dark immunity penalty.

sample_disagreements.label_note: Choice Specs Psychic is a public fail into Dark Houndoom; preserving Alakazam with Hypno is a live defensive line.
ThunderPunch is live neutral coverage; Psychic fails into the public Dark target and is especially bad with choice lock.
Ice Punch is lower-impact than ThunderPunch but still a live move; Psychic is a known failed lock into Houndoom.

mutants.description: Remove the penalty for attempting a fourth Spikes layer.
Remove revealed Rapid Spin hazard-retention discipline.

sample_disagreements.label_note: The active player Starmie has publicly revealed Rapid Spin, so Qwilfish should take a live tempo line instead of adding another layer that can be erased immediately.

mutants.description: Flatten every action to the neutral base score.

sample_disagreements.label_note: both options are close, debuffs are normally weak but gust only does less than 10% so the chip is almost worthless. however multiple sand attacks would be worse, as geodude can switch out to remove all debuffs, so like 3 sand attacks cost 3 turns and geodude switches on turn to remove all debuffs. so sand attack then gust. switching to noctowl is interesting because noctowl can use special attacks to do more damage, but its not worth taking so much damage from geodude. better to sadn attack to weaken geodude then spam gust until you die for safe switch

if quilava is faster then wing attack is better, because otherwise it just goes ember > swords dance > ember and we die

but if scyther is faster, then it goes swords dance > ember > wing attack KOs. 

since wing attack without a boost doesnt Ko quilava, we have to take an ember either way. swords dancing first still kills quilava in 2 turns, taking only one ember, and leaves us with +2

both moves are good, and roll out is iconic. but since geodude has high defense and resists roll out, its best to body slam for extra damage and paralysis chance. once geodude is paralyzed, then switching to roll out is fine

destiny bond is a bad play here because you have to go first and takes down both (and relies on gengar dying, it could set up on you or switch or not do enough damage) when you have a move that does 300% damage! just take the kill, simple
never worry about being cheap, its a legal play. sleep is broken, do it.
